content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Refilling a prescription | Express Scripts by Evernorth

  - url: https://www.express-scripts.com/frequently-asked-questions/refill

  - source: Express Scripts by Evernorth

  - domain: express-scripts.com


  ## Content


  # Refilling a prescription


  This Express Scripts FAQ explains how members refill prescriptions through Express
  Scripts Pharmacy home delivery and how refill timing works.


  To order a home-delivery refill, members sign in to their account and go to **Refill
  Prescriptions** under the Prescriptions menu. The member chooses an available medication
  and places the refill order. Refills can also be ordered through the Express Scripts
  mobile app. Express Scripts says home-delivery refills usually arrive several days
  after the order is received.


  Automatic refills may be available for eligible long-term medications. Members can
  start automatic refills online from the Prescriptions menu by opening the Automatic
  Refills page, selecting Manage Prescriptions, finding an eligible medication, and
  choosing Start Automatic Refills. A medication may also show an automatic-refill
  option when the member is placing an order. After a medication is enrolled, Express
  Scripts reaches out when it is time for a refill or renewal. If needed, the member
  can change the refill processing date to fit their schedule. Members may also call
  the number on their prescription ID card to ask about automatic refills.


  Early refills are generally restricted. Express Scripts explains that early refills
  of medication are usually not allowed to help with patient safety and healthcare
  costs. Standard refill policies generally allow a prescription to be refilled or
  renewed when there should be about 25% to 35% of the current prescription supply
  remaining. These policies are intended to help members get medication on time without
  refilling too early.


  For members who fill one-month supplies at retail and want a longer-term supply,
  Express Scripts says the doctor can send a three-month supply prescription electronically
  to Express Scripts Pharmacy by Evernorth. Members with eligible long-term medications
  can also use automatic refills.


  The FAQ also explains that members can receive text updates about orders by changing
  communication preferences in their account. If there is an urgent issue or if the
  member has questions or concerns, Express Scripts says help is available by calling
  the number on the prescription ID card.


  Automatic refill eligibility is shown in the member website or mobile app with an
  automatic-refill indicator next to eligible medications. If the indicator does not
  appear, the medication is not eligible for automatic refills at that time. In general,
  automatic refills are for long-term medications written for at least a 56-day supply.
  Controlled substances, short-term medications, and prescriptions written for less
  than a 56-day supply are not eligible, and additional criteria may apply.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Refilling a prescription | Express Scripts by Evernorth
url: https://www.express-scripts.com/frequently-asked-questions/refill

Your refill was rejected because it was **too soon**:

- **Prescription:** Maintenance medication  
- **Rejection:** “Refill Too Soon” (code 79)  
- **Last filled:** June 20, 2026  
- **Eligible to resubmit:** July 13, 2026  
- **Current date:** July 16, 2026  
- **Refills remaining:** 2  

Since the eligible date has passed, the next step is to **try submitting the refill again now** through the Express Scripts website or mobile app. Express Scripts generally limits early refills to ensure roughly 25–35% of the current supply remains.

If it is still rejected, call the number on your prescription/insurance ID card or contact the pharmacy and ask them to check the claim. If you’ve run out, will run out before the refill arrives, or the medication was lost or damaged, mention that specifically so they can explain available options.

final_answer:
Your refill was rejected because it was **too soon**:

- **Prescription:** Maintenance medication  
- **Rejection:** “Refill Too Soon” (code 79)  
- **Last filled:** June 20, 2026  
- **Eligible to resubmit:** July 13, 2026  
- **Current date:** July 16, 2026  
- **Refills remaining:** 2  

Since the eligible date has passed, the next step is to **try submitting the refill again now** through the Express Scripts website or mobile app. Express Scripts generally limits early refills to ensure roughly 25–35% of the current supply remains.

If it is still rejected, call the number on your prescription/insurance ID card or contact the pharmacy and ask them to check the claim. If you’ve run out, will run out before the refill arrives, or the medication was lost or damaged, mention that specifically so they can explain available options.

doc_id.normalized_exact: healthcare express scripts coverage reviews pa

text.regex:
(?isx)
^
(?!.*(?:wait|hold\s+off|do\s+not|don't).{0,35}
      (?:until|before).{0,35}
      (?:2026[\s./-]*0?7[\s./-]*13|
         0?7[\s./-]*13[\s./-]*2026|
         (?:july|jul)\s+13(?:st)?(?:\s*,?\s*2026)?))
(?!.*(?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review)
      .{0,70}(?:is|are|was|were|remains?)\s+
      (?:currently\s+)?(?:required|needed|applicable|open|pending))

# Definite refill-timing branch.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    refill[\s_*~`"“”'_-]*too[\s_*~`"“”'_-]*(?:soon|early)
    |
    too[\s_*~`"“”'_-]*(?:soon|early)\s+(?:refill|refill\s+request)
    |
    early[\s_*~`"“”'_-]*refill
    |
    refill[\s_*~`"“”'_-]*timing(?:\s+(?:restriction|issue|limit))?
    |
    (?:refill|claim|request).{0,55}
      (?:submitted|requested|attempted|filled).{0,35}too\s+early
  )
)
(?!.*\b(?:may|might|could|possibly|perhaps|probably|likely)\b
      .{0,80}
      (?:refill[\s_-]*too[\s_-]*(?:soon|early)|
         early[\s_-]*refill|
         refill[\s_-]*timing))

# Explicitly excludes the PA / coverage-review branch.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:no|without)\s+(?:(?:current|active|open|pending)\s+)?
      (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review)
      (?:\s*(?:or|and|/)\s*
        (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review))?
      \s+(?:(?:is|are|was|were)\s+)?(?:currently\s+)?
      (?:required|needed|applicable|indicated|active|open|pending)
    |
    (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review)
      (?:\s*(?:or|and|/)\s*
        (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review))?
      \s+(?:is|are|was|were)\s+(?:currently\s+)?not\s+
      (?:required|needed|applicable|indicated|active|open|pending)
    |
    (?:there\s+(?:is|are)\s+)?no\s+
      (?:current\s+|active\s+)?(?:need|requirement|indication)\s+for\s+
      (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review)
      (?:\s*(?:or|and|/)\s*
        (?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|coverage[\s_-]*review))?
    |
    (?:this|the\s+claim|the\s+rejection)\s+(?:is|was)\s+not\s+
      (?:an?\s+)?(?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|
      coverage[\s_-]*review)\s+
      (?:issue|case|path|rejection|requirement)
    |
    \bnot\s+(?:an?\s+)?(?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|
      coverage[\s_-]*review)\s+
      (?:issue|case|path|rejection|requirement)
    |
    (?:the\s+)?(?:current\s+)?(?:record|records|claim|response|
      information|adjudication).{0,70}
      (?:does|do|did)\s+not\s+(?:show|indicate|support|identify)
      .{0,70}(?:a\s+)?(?:need|requirement|active\s+request|open\s+review)
      .{0,40}(?:for\s+)?(?:prior[\s_-]*auth(?:orization)?|p\.?a\.?|
      coverage[\s_-]*review)
  )
)

# Correct eligible date and the fact that it has passed.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:2026[\s./-]*0?7[\s./-]*13|
     0?7[\s./-]*13[\s./-]*2026|
     (?:july|jul)\s+13(?:st)?(?:\s*,?\s*2026)?|
     13(?:th)?\s+(?:july|jul)(?:\s+2026)?)
)
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:2026[\s./-]*0?7[\s./-]*13|
       0?7[\s./-]*13[\s./-]*2026|
       (?:july|jul)\s+13(?:st)?(?:\s*,?\s*2026)?|
       13(?:th)?\s+(?:july|jul)(?:\s+2026)?)
      .{0,140}
      (?:
        (?:has|had)\s+(?:already\s+)?passed
        |
        (?:is|was)\s+(?:already\s+)?in\s+the\s+past
        |
        (?:was|is)\s+(?:already\s+)?before\s+
          (?:today|the\s+current\s+date)
      )
    |
    (?:today|the\s+current\s+date|current\s+date|
       2026[\s./-]*0?7[\s./-]*16|
       (?:july|jul)\s+16(?:th)?(?:\s*,?\s*2026)?)
      .{0,100}(?:is|falls|comes)?\s*
      (?:after|later\s+than|past|beyond)
      .{0,70}
      (?:2026[\s./-]*0?7[\s./-]*13|
         0?7[\s./-]*13[\s./-]*2026|
         (?:july|jul)\s+13(?:st)?(?:\s*,?\s*2026)?|
         13(?:th)?\s+(?:july|jul)(?:\s+2026)?)
    |
    (?:the\s+)?eligible(?:\s+(?:resubmission|retry|refill))?\s+date
      .{0,70}
      (?:
        (?:has|had)\s+(?:already\s+)?passed
        |
        (?:is|was)\s+(?:already\s+)?in\s+the\s+past
      )
  )
)

# Current next step: retry/resubmit/reprocess now.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:you|the\s+member|the\s+pharmacy)?\s*
      (?:can|should|may|is\s+eligible\s+to)\s+(?:now\s+)?
      (?:retry|try\s+again|resubmit|reprocess|submit|request|refill)
      (?:\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:claim|refill|request|prescription))?
      (?:\s+(?:now|today|again))?
    |
    (?:retry|try\s+again|resubmit|reprocess|submit|request|refill)
      (?:\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:claim|refill|request|prescription))?
      \s+(?:now|today|again)
    |
    ask\s+(?:the\s+)?pharmacy\s+to\s+
      (?:retry|resubmit|reprocess|submit)\s+
      (?:the\s+)?(?:claim|refill|request)\s+(?:now|today|again)
  )
)
.*$
